reviews.review_description: The graphics in this game are amazing. Four levels to play through which is more then enough for a free offering. The controls are free movement with free aiming. The reason I didn't give it a 5 star review is because of lack of control options which I feel is a must for this type of game. You can control player speed which is nice but the game rotates you 180 degrees when you press the touchpad not being able to turn this feature off ruined the game for me. Every-time I got killed was because I accidentally touched the touchpad and got disoriented. You can change weapons by "shaking" your hand which is cool however unless I missed it never explained. Still worth playing through but I wish they had made four more levels and fixed the rotation by touchpad to be optional and I would have paid ten dollars for this game easily. Hats off though for what they did in a relatively short time. The rotation feature! why! lol.

There is a game on the 'big-boy' VR platforms called Compound - a homage to the 16 bit era of fps's. I paid what I thought to be a pricy ticket for (what is) an early access game.  Still no regrets, what blew my mind was how exciting and novel it was to be *IN* a retro-game - against all logic, the pixelated graphics worked so goddam well.
Now the Go has it's own version - and it costs sweet-fanny-addams (=nothing) ! 
So is it good? Hot-potatoes yes! It is as good as Compound - I may even like it better. I would say 'go out and buy it now!' .... but you can't, it's free!!! WHAT?!!

One gripe and it is a gripe I seem to be putting on all reviews - CONTROLLER SUPPORT! If *any* game needs full controller support, it is this one. I want to lie on my big fat back, Bluetooth controller in hand, and a good bottle of bourbon by my side - devs, please make it so (I will buy my own bourbon).

The developer deserves props for this outstanding Old school DOOM inspired fps shooter. The controls are super fluid, the gameplay is intense, and the graphics are an immersive throw back to your favorite FPS of yesteryear. I think the concept of making immersive versions of old school classic games is a BRILLIANT idea! You hit the nostalgia factor and push it I to a whole new realm. PLEASE keep doing this stuff.
This is very well done, Bravo. Visuals are great, environments, etc. A great FPS.
Delivers a great atmosphere by way of fantastic graphics and sound design. Movement takes a second to get used to but is far superior to the teleport system. Loved it!

This game really is bringing the promise of VR gaming to the Go. I really love the design choices and overall aesthetic. Brilliant usage of sprites. I will be keeping an eye on this dev and buy any future Go games that they produce.

reviews.review_title: Doom. Quake. Compound. On the GO!
reviews.review_description: Wow this is a great experience and worth paying for, I can't believe it is free. Very polished, well designed first person shooter with free locomotion movement via touch pad. Also you can turn around by clicking touchpad, and change weapons by pointing controller up. Thanks Avant Games and Garage Collective for your efforts! I hope to see more from your collaboration! Awesomeness!

Some things i'd like to see:
- Option to use head oriented or controller oriented walking
-Feedback in the menus (highlighting / sounds when hovering and clicking)
-MSAA might need to be turned on 4x (lots of jagged edges on long straight lines)
- enemies need AI work - there is no warning, and there is no way to 'dodge' shots - most enemies that shoot will inevitebly hit you - granted staying alive isnt a problem, but its not satisfying to see an enemy and not be able to avoid damage.  Slow down enemy projectiles maybe?  Also, most enemies dont move either at all, or in interesting ways.
- 180 turn is pretty useless, very disorienting and I click the touchpad inadvertently occasionally.  "Look to turn" might work better for seated play (somewhat like Rigs on PSVR).
- There may need to be a map on the pause menu

Its a good start, and I'd like to see where this goes
I'm shocked we don't have more free movement FPS games. This one has a great retro look and it's like stepping into an Aliens franchise PSOne game. I love it.

reviews.review_title: This is actually the best game ever.
reviews.review_description: I’m being completely serious. This game NEEDS more attention. Not even Drop Dead can match up to how perfect this game really is. This is exactly the type of game that I’ve been looking for on the Oculus Go for a VERY long time. The action is plentiful. The movement is smooth. The guns are awesome. The decision to style this game after Doom/Quake was an AMAZING idea. They mask how bad the graphics are by making everything pixels. That is what makes the graphics look amazing on top of all the action. Even the soundtrack is perfect. JUST TAKE MY MONEY ALREADY!

Everything is too big, unless we are a 8 feet giant. Is it to much to ask for my head to be actually close to a human average height? my feet are floating 3 feet above the ground. Hey DEV! have you played your own game in VR? Scale the world by 0.75 and it will probably look and feel right. Otherwise it is a fantastic game, but I can't play because I feel disoriented by the WRONG world scale. This is a cancer of Oculus Go.

reviews.review_title: best single player shooter on go
reviews.review_description: Great little game, best single player shooter i’ve played on the go yet, and its free too!
This game is amazing!!! How is this free?? Holy S*%T!!!!
You can play it with a second single-handed bluetooth controller (for movement), and for that alone I'm giving it 5 stars. (And because it's free)

Best free game

It’s just like DOOM

Please add multiplayer
This is very good for a free game.  Best played with a swivel chair.
short but gold tho

First... I want to say to the 3 star review that clicking on both sides let you turn 90degrees. Hopefully that can help you enjoy the game :).

The game is visually incredible. The 2D sprites mixed with the 3D with pixelated textures throw you back in your classics (such as doom3D) with a modern twist to it.

The sounds are also well designed. The atmosphere and enemy sounds are great. Voices for missions are well done.

The controls are perfect. We are getting used to the free locomotion (most games are using it) and the addition of "point over head" to change gun is appreciated.

Old school shooter, old school visuals, fresh design and it's free. Any 30+ years old should play this game... It will bring back old memories! And 30- should give it a try! It's a really well done game.

An impressive game for free, that feels better than many paid games. You should definitely try it if you like shooters.

One of the things that could make the game even better, as another guy in a review said: bourbon and controller support.

This is an excellent and well produced game. Takes me back to the days of Doom !  Quake, and other great games.
The graphics are great, the music is atmospheric, the gameplay is absorbing. And all for free !
